A '''nightshade thorn''' was a [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] thorny [[Plant/Legends|plant]] that grew on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]] stepped on a nightshade thorn and was poisoned. The Ewoks [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] and [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] used the [[bridge crystal]] to cross the [[Gorge of Fire]], a [[Fire/Legends|fiery]] canyon and recover some [[Glowing nectar|sunnydew nectar]], that was the only medicine that could save [[Gender/Legends|her]], stealing it from [[Gantu]], an [[ogre]].

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Look Wicket! Kneesaa stepped on a ''poisonus'' nightshade thorn.|Teebo, to Wicket Wystri Warrick when referring to the thorn Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka had stepped on|<ref name="E1" />}}
A nightshade thorn was a [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] thorny [[Plant/Legends|plant]] found on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]],<ref name="E1" /> a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="GATORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> The nightshade thorn was brown and its thorns wounded those who stepped on them. The only known cure to its poison was the [[Glowing nectar|sunnydew nectar]].<ref name="E1" />

==History==
In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date note">{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, which include the events of [[Ewoks (1985) 1|''Ewoks'' 1]] in which a nightshade thorn appears, to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the young [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], while walking in a [[forest]] with fellow Ewoks [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] and [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]], stepped on a nightshade thorn and was poisoned. After being brought back to [[Gender/Legends|her]] [[Bright Tree Village|village]], the [[Shaman/Legends|medicine man]] [[Logray/Legends|Logray]] tried in vain to heal her.<ref name="E1" />

The only way to heal the princess was to use the [[Glowing nectar|sunnydew nectar]], that was possessed by an [[ogre]] named [[Gantu]] and guarded beyond the [[Gorge of Fire]], a [[Fire/Legends|firey]] canyon that devide Endor. Warrick and Teebo stole the [[bridge crystal]], a crystal that allowed to create the [[Rainbow Bridge]], and used it to cross the Gorge of Fire. On the other side of the Gorge, they met [[Zandor Rocker]]s, a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] species that looked similar to rocks, and they helped the two Ewoks to face Gantu. During the fight, however, the bridge crystal was destroyed and all the remaining sunnydew nectar was poured on to the orge. The two Ewoks returned to the village using a tunnel excavated by the Zandor Rockers, and they realized that some of the necater fell on a [[Flower/Legends|flower]] that Wicket had in his bag. When the princess sniffed the flower, she was immediatly healed.<ref name="E1" />

==Behind the scenes==
The nightshade thorn appeared in [[Ewoks (1985) 1|''Ewoks'' 1]], the first issue of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] comic book from [[Marvel Comics]]. The story was written by [[David Manak]] and illustrated by [[Warren Kremer]].<ref name="E1" /> It was first published on [[January 29]], [[1985]].<ref name="Marvel Age">{{BookCite|title=[[Marvel Age]]|number=Vol. 1 No. 25|publisher=[[Marvel Comics]] Group|date=[[April]] [[1985]]}}</ref>
